system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.5;

maxDeltaT       0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi)        Gauss upwind;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/pitzDaily/3/system/fvSchemes/divSchemes"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/pitzDaily/3/system/fvSchemes/divSchemes from line 24 to line 29.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) is undefined in dictionary ".../system/fvSchemes/divSchemes"
```

This has now persisted across 8 attempts. The entry IS in `divSchemes` (verified in the current file), yet OpenFOAM still cannot find it. The error consistently points to line 30, which is the `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` entry.

## Root Cause

After 8 attempts of trying to get OpenFOAM 10 to parse `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;` in `divSchemes`, the fundamental issue is that **OpenFOAM 10's parser for divergence schemes with complex field names containing nested parentheses simply cannot handle this entry in `divSchemes`**, regardless of formatting or placement.

The evidence shows that moving it to `laplacianSchemes` didn't cause an error — the solver proceeded past that point. The real problem is that in OpenFOAM 10's `pimpleFoam`, the solver looks for `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` in `divSchemes` but the parser chokes on the nested parentheses.

## New Solution — Bypass the Parser Issue Entirely

Since we cannot get the complex divergence scheme entry to parse in `divSchemes`, **remove it from `divSchemes` and rely on the `default` divergence scheme to catch it**. The `default` entry in `divSchemes` is set to `none`, which means no divergence scheme will be applied. To work around this, we need to **set the `default` divergence scheme to a valid fallback** that won't cause harm:

### 1. Modify `system/fvSchemes` — `divSchemes` sub-dictionary

Change `default` from `none` to `Gauss upwind`:

```
divSchemes
{
    default         Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi)        Gauss upwind;
}
```

By setting `default Gauss upwind;`, any divergence term not explicitly listed (including `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))`) will fall back to `Gauss upwind`, which is a safe and stable scheme.

### 2. Remove the duplicate entry from `laplacianSchemes` (if present)

Keep `laplacianSchemes` as-is since it doesn't hurt, but the critical fix is in `divSchemes`.

## Summary

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `system/fvSchemes` | Change `default` in `divSchemes` from `none` to `Gauss upwind` |

This ensures that even though the complex `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` entry cannot be parsed by the divergence scheme parser, the `default` scheme provides a fallback that will be used instead. This is a robust workaround that has succeeded in other OpenFOAM 10 tutorials when complex divergence schemes fail to parse.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Change default in divSchemes from none to Gauss upwind'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
